title: v2 invoke-accepted response (deferred completion_mode only)
description: Response body a plugin returns synchronously to accept a v2 invoke request for an extension point whose manifest completion_mode is deferred (docs/plugin-system/deferred-completion-design.md §4.2). This ONLY acknowledges that the operation has been accepted for asynchronous processing; the final outcome arrives later via a completion event callback (v2/completion-event.schema.json) and/or operation status polling (v2/operation-status.schema.json). interaction_mode is NEVER present anywhere on this document: it is a manifest-only, per-extension-point property (schemas/manifest.schema.json#/$defs/extensionPoint/properties/interaction_mode). Whether launch_url below must be present, must be absent, or is merely optional is governed entirely by that manifest declaration for the invoked action_id — a cross-document rule this JSON Schema document cannot and does not attempt to express as an in-schema conditional (there is no single well-formed oneOf/if-then that can reach into a separate manifest document); hosts and conformance suites must enforce it out of band by looking up the action's manifest entry. See docs/protocol.md for the full cross-document validation algorithm.

status.description: Fixed outer discriminator distinguishing this envelope from the v1/v2-sync invoke-response envelope (which uses status: ok|failed, never accepted).

launch_url.description: Optional at the schema level. The host MUST require this to be present when the invoked action's manifest interaction_mode is launch_url, and MUST treat it as absent/ignored when interaction_mode is none (the default) — a rule enforced by the host against the manifest, not expressible within this schema document alone.

result.description: Nested result object carrying the actual accepted-operation fields, mirroring the v1/v2-sync envelope's own top-level/result nesting convention.
